title: Finding the Slope
text: Find the slope of the line that passes through the points $$(-1,4)$$ and $$(5,2)$$.

title: Using the Formula
text: The slope $$m$$ of a line passing through two points (x1,y1​) and (x2​,y2​) is given by the formula: $$m=\frac{y2-y1}{x2-x1}$$. Substituting points into the formula, what is the value of the slope $$m$$?

title: Getting Point Slope Form
text: The point-slope form of a line is: $$y-y1=m(x-x1)$$. Using point $$(-1,4)$$ and slope $$m=\frac{-1}{3}$$, we can get the point-slope form of the line.
